suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that kernel fusion on gfx906 for decode path (skip-RMSNorm + GEMV) is counterproductive due to the lack of cooperative groups. Each block must independently load all K activation elements for normalization, creating O(N*K) redundant HBM reads. The correctness is achievable but latency regresses ~1.3-2x. Workers should not attempt this pattern again without verifying the shape is bandwidth-limited rather than compute-limited.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fused-skip-rmsnorm-gemv: at K=5120, N=4096/11008, fused kernel was 1.33-2.05x slower than separate kernels. Documented in architecture.md under Kernel Fusion section.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that FP16 GEMM at Qwen3 27B prefill shapes (M=128, N≥6144, K≥5120) is compute-dominated on gfx906, achieving ~6-7 TFLOPS (~50% efficiency). Epilogue fusions that save HBM traffic (up to 12MB per FFN call) do not produce measurable wall-clock improvement at these shapes. SiLU epilogue fusion is only beneficial for significantly larger M (M≥2048) where the kernel becomes more bandwidth-limited.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fused-silu-prefill-gemm: Strategy B at M=128, N=11008, K=5120 showed 0.987x (neutral). Strategy A showed 0.777x (slower) due to 2x WG grid overhead. Documented in architecture.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: library/architecture.md already has detailed documentation of both kernel fusion patterns (fused-skip-rmsnorm-gemv and fused-silu-prefill-gemm sections added by prior worker during implementation). No new library updates needed.
